description: Known-answer test for the canonical signing bytes of a SignedCommand (lock / unlock). The Ed25519 signature covers the RFC 8785 (JCS) canonical bytes of the command with its `sig` field removed. Both independent implementations MUST produce these exact bytes: the parent `CommandSigner.signingBytes` (parent-kmp, Bouncy Castle side) and the child `CommandVerifier.canonicalBody` (child-android, libsodium side). JCS rules pinned here: keys sorted by UTF-16 code unit (child_device_id < issued_at < type < v), no whitespace, integer `issued_at`/`v`, `sig` excluded, and `v:1` PRESENT (a defaulted value is part of the signed bytes — encodeDefaults=true, ADR-047 D1). If either port drifts (key order, whitespace, `sig` inclusion, number formatting, or a dropped `v`) it produces different bytes and rejects every command with SIG_FAIL / MALFORMED.

notes.v_is_present: v:1 is a defaulted field on the parent SignedCommand but is signed and MUST appear on the wire (ADR-047 D1). The child SignedCommand.v has NO default: a v-less body is rejected MALFORMED (fail-closed, ADR-047 D3), never re-defaulted.
notes.cross_impl: Consumed by parent-kmp shared/commonTest SignedCommandCanonicalVectorTest (CommandSigner.signingBytes) and child-android CommandCanonicalVectorTest (CommandVerifier.canonicalBody). Both MUST equal canonical_bytes_utf8.

expect: BOTH ports emit canonical_bytes_utf8 verbatim; any drift → SIG_FAIL / MALFORMED (fail-closed)
